name: sec-review-marketplace
description: Single-plugin marketplace distributing sec-review: a multi-lane cybersecurity audit plugin covering web services, browser extensions, Rust crates, Go projects, mobile apps (Android + iOS), desktop apps (Linux + macOS + Windows), Kubernetes manifests, Terraform/Pulumi IaC, GitHub Actions workflows, and virtualization / container runtimes (Docker, Podman / Quadlet, libvirt / QEMU / KVM, Apple Containers, UTM) — with live CVE enrichment and citation-grounded fixes.

plugins.name: sec-review
plugins.description: Eighteen-agent cybersecurity review pipeline (sec-expert + finding-triager + cve-enricher + report-writer + fourteen tool-lane runners: sast, dast, webext, rust, android, ios, linux, macos, windows, k8s, iac, gh-actions, virt, go) with model pinning — sonnet for code reasoning, haiku for high-volume CVE feed I/O and tool dispatch. Multi-stack dispatch is the default: when a project satisfies ≥2 inventory keys (Tauri = Rust + webext + desktop OS; Flutter = Android + iOS; React-Native = Android + iOS + webext), all applicable runners dispatch in parallel and findings render in separate per-lane sections. The `/sec-review` slash command accepts `--only=<lanes>` / `--skip=<lanes>` flags for selective dispatch; the report includes a per-lane summary table and lane-filter metadata. Citation-grounded expertise across databases, frameworks, webservers (incl. Windows/IIS), proxies, frontend, browser extensions (Chrome MV3 + Firefox AMO), Rust/Cargo + unsafe surface, Android (manifest + data + runtime), iOS (Info.plist + codesign), desktop Linux (systemd + sandboxing + packaging), desktop macOS (hardened runtime + TCC + .pkg/Sparkle), desktop Windows (PE hardening + Authenticode + AppLocker/WDAC + MSI/MSIX), Kubernetes admission, Terraform/Pulumi IaC, GitHub Actions, virtualization / alternative-container-runtimes (Docker daemon, Podman / Quadlet, libvirt / QEMU / KVM, Apple Containers, UTM), Go (stdlib security + module ecosystem + web frameworks Gin/Echo/Fiber/Chi/gRPC), auth, TLS, containers, secrets, and supply-chain — all primary-source-cited. Live CVE enrichment from NVD 2.0, OSV.dev (native for crates.io/Maven/NuGet/PyPI/npm/Go/RubyGems/Packagist; best-effort via GHSA for CocoaPods/SwiftPM/Debian), and GHSA with CISA KEV cross-reference. Structured skipped-list distinguishes twelve canonical clean-skip reasons (no-apk/no-bundle/no-pkg/no-debian-source/no-elf/no-systemd-unit/no-pe/no-containerfile/no-libvirt-xml/requires-macos-host/requires-systemd-host/requires-windows-host) plus no-notary-profile from tool failures. `references/COVERAGE.md` is the single source of truth enumerating every lane, ecosystem, and skip reason. Per-run token-cost accounting; offline-degradation drills for CVE feeds and all tool lanes; prioritized markdown report with verbatim-quoted primary-source fixes.
